content: Combining the task-agnostic iterative self-feedback framework of Self-Refine with the multiple inconsistent perspectives at the core of emerging LLM self-reflection mechanisms creates a new paradigm where LLMs can iteratively refine any initial output by cross-checking against their own divergent solving perspectives, without any external supervision or training. This approach addresses the key limitation of standard Self-Refine, which often gets stuck in local optima because it only critiques output from a single default frame of reference.
